titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Landmarks Foundation Records
titlestmt.author: by Gabriela Redwine

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday November 10, 2025 at 23:15

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Landmarks Foundation records include correspondence, printed material, photographs, research materials, computer files, and other materials relating to the foundation and its work across the world. The foundation's work in Turkey, Mexico, and Costa Rica is particularly well documented in the project files. Also present is a small amount of correspondence and other materials related to Sam Green's work and life prior to the creation of the foundation.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Landmarks Foundation Records include correspondence, printed material, photographs, slides, research materials, computer files, and other materials relating to the organization's work to preserve sacred cultural heritage sites around the world. The foundation's work on sites in Turkey, Mexico, and Costa Rica and relationships with local individuals and organizations is particularly well documented in the project files with letters, faxes, photographs, and other papers. Green's collaboration with faculty and students at the University of Pennsylvania on different preservation projects is also well documented in correspondence and project proposals. The project files also provide insight into Green's approach to researching and scouting potential future projects for the organization. Administrative files include correspondence and other papers related to Green's efforts to raise funds, develop projects, and build relationships in support of the organization's work. The collection also includes a small amount of correspondence and other materials related to his work and life prior to the creation of the foundation.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. Box 13 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information. Box 14 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Landmarks Foundation Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscsript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Landmarks Foundation Records. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: Series I. Project files, Series II. Administrative files, Series III. Computer media and audiovisual materials.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Landmarks Foundation was a non-profit organization founded in 1997 by Samuel Adams Green to conserve sacred sites and landscapes around the world. Their motto was "protecting sacred sites globally." The foundation directed funding and technical expertise to local groups that could not protect their cultural heritage sites without assistance. Originally based in New York City, the foundation relocated to Massachussetts after Green's death in 2011 and continued under the leadership of Haley Bain (formerly O'Neil) before closing.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Samuel Adams Green (1940-2011) was an American art curator, socialite, preservationist, and founder of the Landmarks Foundation. He served as director of the Institute of Contemporary Art, Philadelphia, from 1964-1968, where he curated Andy Warhol's 1965 Retrospective. From 1968-1969 he worked as a Cultural Consultant for New York City's Office of Cultural Affairs, and from 1968-1972 served as Special Projects Director for the International Fund for Monuments (UNESCO) in New York. He also worked as a private art consultant, served as assistant to the British photographer Cecil Beaton, and was friends with Greta Garbo, John Lennon, Yoko Ono, and other notable artists, cultural figures, and socialites. In 1996 Green launched the Cultural Heritage Foundation New York, which evolved into the Landmarks Foundation in 1997. He devoted the last decades of his life to preserving sacred cultural heritage sites and landscapes around the world.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into subgroupings by country, and then alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Project files series consists of over nine boxes of materials that document with correspondence, photographs, reports, clippings, and other papers the organization's work in a number of countries across the globe. The files include documentation of both successful and unrealized projects, as well as Green's travel prior to the founding of the Landmarks Foundation.

did.unittitle: Costa Rica, Diquís Delta stone spheres
Photographs and photocopies of documents concerning restoration
Project proposal (Conservación Humana AC and Monument Rescue Trust)
Santa Fe: follow up to Sol y Sombre
Santa Fe: guest list and correspondence
Santa Fe: Sol y Sombre -- Miller
Radar Imaging Survey of the Angkor Eco-Site

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Administrative files series consists of three boxes that document with correspondence, newsletters, articles, and other papers the work of raising funds and building an organization.

did.unittitle: Funding, W. H. Donner Foundation, grant application
Museum of World Religions, opening ceremony (Taipei)
Sacred Sites conference (International Conference on the Global Preservation of Sacred Sites, Taipei)
World Monuments Fund/Monument Rescue Fund, certificate of incorporation
Series III. Computer media and audiovisual materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Computer media and audiovisual materials series includes 14 optical media with files related to different Landmarks projects as well as the organization's "archive." The 2 videocassette (VHS) tapes are: (1) Living Jewel of Bhutan: The Restoration of Gangteng Monastery and (2) a tape labeled "Celebrity Deathmatch: Yoko vs. Fabio...The Making of a Bestseller (Documentary about Albert Goldman's Lennon Bio) RUFF CUT + FINAL VERSION), stored in a box labeled "Greta Garbo: The Lone Star..."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
